focus: Austrian family Mittelstand, no P&L filed — exercises revenue triangulation from abridged balance sheet, succession read, niche moat.
rawData:
FIRMENBUCH EXTRACT (Austria), retrieved June 2026:
Alpenstahl Maschinenbau GmbH, FN 87654x, registered seat: Wörgl, Tirol. Founded 1989.
Share capital: EUR 145,000. Shareholders: Johann Gruber (75%), Maria Gruber (25%).
Managing directors (Geschäftsführer): Johann Gruber (born 1961, appointed 1989), Dipl.-Ing. Stefan Leitner (born 1978, appointed March 2023).
Prokura: Maria Gruber (since 1994).
Note: In January 2025 a holding company 'Gruber Beteiligungs GmbH' (FN 612345p, shareholders Johann Gruber 75%, Maria Gruber 25%) was interposed as sole shareholder of Alpenstahl Maschinenbau GmbH.

FILED ACCOUNTS (abridged, small GmbH regime — no P&L disclosed):
FY2023 (filed July 2024, on time): Total assets EUR 14.2m; Fixed assets EUR 6.8m (of which land & buildings EUR 3.9m, technical equipment & machinery EUR 2.4m); Equity EUR 8.9m (equity ratio 62.7%); Bank liabilities EUR 1.1m; Average employees during year: 118.
FY2022: Total assets EUR 13.1m; Equity EUR 7.8m (59.5%); Average employees: 112.
FY2021: Total assets EUR 12.6m; Equity EUR 7.1m (56.3%); Average employees: 109.

LAND REGISTER / CHARGES: One Höchstbetragshypothek (maximum amount mortgage) EUR 1.5m in favour of Raiffeisenbank Wörgl, registered 2014, on the factory property.

WEBSITE (alpenstahl.at, retrieved June 2026): 'Family-owned since 1989. We develop and build special-purpose machinery for the wood processing industry — fully automated sawline feeding systems, log sorting plants and residual wood handling. Over 400 plants delivered in 23 countries. Export share approx. 70%. Our customers include leading European sawmill groups.' Careers page lists 6 open positions: 2x mechatronics service technician (international travel), 1x PLC software engineer (Beckhoff TwinCAT), 1x mechanical design engineer, 1x project manager, 1x apprentice. News section: October 2025 'Commissioning of the new assembly hall (1,800 m²) completed'; March 2024 'Alpenstahl delivers its largest sorting plant to date to a Scandinavian customer'.

LINKEDIN: Company page, 87 followers, 64 employees listed on LinkedIn. Stefan Leitner's profile: 'Geschäftsführer Technik & Vertrieb at Alpenstahl' since 2023; previously 12 years at a large Austrian plant engineering group, last role Head of Sales Timber Technology.

LOCAL PRESS (Tiroler Tageszeitung, Nov 2025, interview with Johann Gruber on the company's 35th anniversary): 'We have grown every year since 2015 except corona year 2020. The order book reaches well into 2027. My daughter works in the company management, and with Stefan Leitner we have brought technical leadership in from outside. I am 64 — of course one starts to think about how the life's work continues.'
humanReviewHints: Revenue should triangulate to roughly EUR 20-33m (118 employees x EUR 180-280k specialised machinery benchmark), cross-checked against the asset base; the small-GmbH abridged filing is interesting: revenue may exceed the EUR 10m small threshold — engine should notice size-class tension and reason about it rather than ignore it.
Equity ratio rising 56->63% with no capital increase = retained profits; margin read should lean healthy.

Owner motivation is the heart of this case: founder 64, external GF hired 2023, holding interposed Jan 2025 = strong pre-sale/succession structuring signals. A good dossier commits to 'sale or structured succession within ~2-4 years'.
Moat: niche special-purpose machinery + installed base of 400 plants (service/spares) + qualified-supplier status with sawmill groups.
Deal-killers should include owner/key-person dependence and possible customer concentration (large sawmill groups).
